title: Crepidifolium tenuifolium
intro: Crepidifolium tenuifolium, also known as Kōki or Koki, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and infections. The bioactive constituents of C. tenuifolium include sesquiterpenes, diterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, C. tenuifolium is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and fever.
taxonomy: Crepidifolium tenuifolium belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread flowering plant families in the world. This family is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. The genus Crepidifolium is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae, which includes plants with small, daisy-like flowers.
